items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices` **Service directory:** `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/` **Triggered by:** PR #57510 / commit 8ba2ed9 by `@azure-sdk` (version bumped to 1.6.0-beta.2 in #58296) ## What Changed PR #57510 upgraded the API version to `2026-01-15-preview` and introduced several new types and properties: - New **`FoundryAutoUpgrade`** model ‚Äď configures auto-upgrade behavior for AI Foundry accounts (fields: `Mode`, `PlannedByMicrosoft`, `StatusReason`, `ScheduledOn`) - New **`FoundryAutoUpgradeMode`** enum (`Enabled`, `Disabled`, etc.) - New **`CognitiveServicesQuotaScopeType`** enum - **`CognitiveServicesAccountProperties`** gained three new properties: `FoundryAutoUpgrade`, `AllowProjectManagement`, `DefaultProject` - **`CognitiveServicesManagedNetworkSettingsResource`** received ~100 lines of new operations - `ModelSkuCapacityProperties` and `ServiceAccountUsage` each gained new properties ## Gaps Found - The `1.6.0-beta.1` CHANGELOG entry only says _"Upgraded api-version to 2026-01-15-preview"_ ‚Äď none of the new types (`FoundryAutoUpgrade`, `FoundryAutoUpgradeMode`, `CognitiveServicesQuotaScopeType`) or new properties on existing models are mentioned - `README.md` **Examples** section contains only a link to general management samples; there are no package-specific code snippets for creating/managing a Cognitive Services account - `README.md` **Key concepts** section links to general Azure SDK docs but does not list or describe the resource types surfaced by this package (accounts, deployments, projects, connections, RAI policies, managed network settings, etc.) - None of the AI Foundry-related features added since v1.5.0 (Projects, Connections, CapabilityHosts) are described or demonstrated in the README
ūüďź Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1 ‚Äď `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/CHANGELOG.md` Edit the `1.6.0-beta.1` section to list the new types and properties: ```markdown ## 1.6.0-beta.1 (2026-04-17) ### Features Added - Upgraded api-version to `2026-01-15-preview`. - Added `FoundryAutoUpgrade` model and `FoundryAutoUpgradeMode` enum to configure AI Foundry auto-upgrade behavior on a Cognitive Services account. - Added `CognitiveServicesQuotaScopeType` enum for quota scope classification. - Added `FoundryAutoUpgrade`, `AllowProjectManagement`, and `DefaultProject` properties to `CognitiveServicesAccountProperties`. - Enhanced `CognitiveServicesManagedNetworkSettingsResource` with additional management operations. - Added new properties to `ModelSkuCapacityProperties` and `ServiceAccountUsage`. ### Other Changes - Upgraded dependent Azure.Core to 1.53.0. - Upgraded dependent Azure.ResourceManager to 1.14.0. ``` #### File 2 ‚Äď `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/README.md` Update the **Key concepts** section to enumerate resource types, and replace the generic **Examples** section with a concrete snippet placeholder. Replace the current `## Key concepts` section with: ```markdown ## Key concepts The `Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices` library manages the following resource types: | Resource | Description | |---|---| | `CognitiveServicesAccountResource` | Top-level Cognitive Services account | | `CognitiveServicesAccountDeploymentResource` | Model deployment within an account | | `CognitiveServicesProjectResource` | AI Foundry project scoped under an account | | `CognitiveServicesConnectionResource` | Connection attached to an account or project | | `CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostResource` | Capability host attached to an account | | `CognitiveServicesManagedNetworkSettingsResource` | Managed virtual network settings for an account | | `RaiPolicyResource` / `RaiBlocklistResource` | Responsible AI policy and blocklist resources | | `CommitmentPlanResource` | Commitment plan for reserved capacity | ``` Replace the current `## Examples` section with: ```markdown ## Examples ### Create a Cognitive Services account ```C# Snippet:CognitiveServices_CreateAccount ``` ``` (The snippet tooling will fill in the code once a sample test is written ‚Äď see Step 2.) ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Create a new test file at `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/tests/Samples/Sample1_CognitiveServicesAccount.cs`: ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System.Threading.Tasks; using Azure.Core.TestFramework; using Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices.Models; using Azure.ResourceManager.Resources; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices.Tests.Samples { public class Sample1_CognitiveServicesAccount : SamplesBase { [Test] [SyncOnly] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task CreateCognitiveServicesAccount() { #region Snippet:CognitiveServices_CreateAccount // Authenticate and get a subscription ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); // Get (or create) a resource group ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = (await subscription.GetResourceGroups() .GetAsync("myResourceGroup")).Value; // Define account properties var accountData = new CognitiveServicesAccountData(AzureLocation.EastUS) { Kind = "OpenAI", Sku = new CognitiveServicesSku("S0"), Properties = new CognitiveServicesAccountProperties { CustomSubDomainName = "my-openai-account", PublicNetworkAccess = ServiceAccountPublicNetworkAccess.Enabled, } }; // Create the account CognitiveServicesAccountCollection accounts = resourceGroup.GetCognitiveServicesAccounts(); ArmOperation operation = await accounts.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, "myAccount", accountData); CognitiveServicesAccountResource account = operation.Value; Console.WriteLine($"Created account: {account.Data.Name}"); #endregion } } } ``` Place the snippet placeholder in `README.md` as shown in Step 1. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README currently contains all required sections but some are thin: | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started ‚Üí Install the package | ‚úÖ Present | | Getting started ‚Üí Prerequisites | ‚úÖ Present | | Getting started ‚Üí Authenticate the client | ‚úÖ Present | | Key concepts | ‚ö†ÔłŹ Links to generic docs ‚Äď needs package-specific resource list | | Examples | ‚ö†ÔłŹ Links to generic samples ‚Äď needs at least one inline snippet | | Troubleshooting | ‚úÖ Present | | Next steps | ‚úÖ Present | | Contributing | ‚úÖ Present | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for tone and formatting. ### Step 4: Validate Run in order ‚Äď each must succeed before proceeding: 1. `dotnet build sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 cognitiveservices` ‚ÄĒ injects snippet code from the new sample file into README placeholders; verify with `git diff` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 cognitiveservices` ‚ÄĒ only needed if public API surface changed (it did not change in this doc-only update) 5. `dotnet format sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [CognitiveServices] Docs: Update CHANGELOG and README for 1.6.0-beta.1 new features
